zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Bowden於大約1490年出生在英國戴文時，她的父親Sir John Bowden27歲，母親Christina Fulbrook26歲。她和Nicholas Berry至少有1個兒子和5個女兒。她去世於大約1530年在英國戴文貝利納波爾享年42歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Bowden于大约1490年出生在英国戴文时，她的父亲Sir John Bowden27岁，母亲Christina Fulbrook26岁。她和Nicholas Berry至少有1个儿子和5个女儿。她去世于大约1530年在英国戴文貝利納波爾享年42岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bowden
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places called Bowden or Bowdon. Bowden in Devon and Derbyshire and Bowdon in Cheshire are named with Old English boga ‘bow’ + dūn ‘hill’, i.e. ‘hill shaped like a bow’; one in Leicestershire (Bugedone in Domesday Book) comes, according to Ekwall, from the Old English personal name Būga (masculine) or Bucge (feminine) + dūn. There are also Scottish places of this name, but there are comparatively few bearers of the surname Bowden north of the border. In England, the surname is found most frequently in Lancashire and in the West Country. In Devon and Cornwall there has been some confusion with the Norman personal name Baldwin .
English: topographic name for someone who lived at the top of a hill, from Middle English buve dun ‘above the hill’ (Old English būfan dūne, as in the placename Bowden, Wiltshire).
Scottish: habitational name from Bowden in Roxburghshire, named from Old English bōthl ‘dwelling-house’ + Old English denu ‘valley’.

variants.name: Baldwin
meanings: English and North German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements bald ‘bold, brave’ + wine ‘friend’, which was extremely popular among the Normans and in Flanders in the early Middle Ages. It was the personal name of the Crusader who in 1100 became the first Christian king of Jerusalem, and of four more Crusader kings of Jerusalem. It was also borne by Baldwin, Count of Flanders (1172–1205), leader of the Fourth Crusade, who became first Latin Emperor of Constantinople (1204). In North America, this surname has absorbed Dutch forms such as Boudewijn.
Irish: surname adopted in Donegal by bearers of the Gaelic surname Ó Maolagáin (see Milligan ), due to association of Gaelic maol ‘bald, hairless’ with English bald.
History: A John Baldwin from Buckinghamshire, England, arrived in the US in 1638 and settled in Milford, CT.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Bowden was born about 1490, in Devon, England, her father, Sir John Bowden, was 27 and her mother, Christina Fulbrook, was 26. She had at least 1 son and 5 daughters with Nicholas Berry. She died about 1530, in Berrynarbor, Devon, England, at the age of 42.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Bowden est née environ 1490, à Devon, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Bowden, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Christina Fulbrook, avait 26 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 5 filles avec Nicholas Berry. Elle est décédée environ 1530, à Berrynarbor, Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 42 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Bowden um 1490 in Devon, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John Bowden 27 und ihre Mutter Christina Fulbrook 26. Sie hatte mit Nicholas Berry mindestens 1 Sohn und 5 Töchter. Sie starb um 1530 im Alter von 42 Jahren in Berrynarbor, Devon, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Bowden è nata il circa 1490 nel Devon, England, sua padre, Sir John Bowden, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Christina Fulbrook, aveva 26 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio e 5 figlie con Nicholas Berry. Morì circa 1530, nel Berrynarbor, Devon, England, all'età di 42 anni.

ja.Berrynarbor, Devon, England: イギリスデボンバーリナルボル
ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Bowdenが約1490年にイギリスデボンで生まれた時，父Sir John Bowdenは27歳，母Christina Fulbrookは26歳であった。彼女はNicholas Berryとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と5人の娘をもうけた。彼女は約1530年にイギリスデボンバーリナルボルで42歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Bowden(이)가 대략 1490년 영국 데폰에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Bowden(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Christina Fulbrook(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 Nicholas Berry(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1530년 영국 데폰 베리나르볼에서 42세의 나이로 사망했다.

mg.about 1490–about 1530: tany ho any amin'ny 1490–tany ho any amin'ny 1530

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Bowden nasceu aproximadamente 1490, em Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Bowden, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Christina Fulbrook, tinha 26 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho e 5 filhas com Nicholas Berry. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1530, em Berrynarbor, Devon, Inglaterra, com 42 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Bowden родилась около 1490 г. в местности девон, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Bowden было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Christina Fulbrook, 26. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын и 5 дочерей от Nicholas Berry. Она умерла около 1530 г. в местности бирринарбор, девон, Англия в возрасте 42 лет.

Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 5 hijas con Nicholas Berry. Murió aproximadamente 1530, en Berrynarbor, Devon, Inglaterra, a la edad de 42 años.
